name: Parque Natural Despeñaperros — circular de gravel desde Coto Sierra del Viso
region_intro: Touring cycling around Viso Del Marqués offers diverse landscapes in the province of Ciudad Real, Spain. The region is situated in the foothills of the Sierra Morena mountain range, providing varied terrain for cyclists. It features rolling trails, rugged mountains, and sun-soaked valleys, transitioning from the La Mancha plains. This environment is suitable for touring cyclists seeking challenging routes. Best touring cycling routes around Viso Del Marqués The most popular touring cycling route is Parque Natural Despeñaperros — circular de gravel desde Coto Sierra del Viso, a 24.3 miles (39.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 23 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural park, offering gravel sections. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Roman Bridge in Despeñaperros – La Aliseda Recreation Area loop from Miranda del Rey, a difficult 26.9 miles (43.3 km) path. This route includes the Roman Bridge and the La Aliseda Recreation Area within the Despeñaperros region. Local touring cyclists also love the La Aliseda Recreation Area – El Empedraillo loop from Santa Elena, a 28.7 miles (46.2 km) trail leading through the Sierra Morena foothills, often completed in about 4 hours 20 minutes. Touring cycling around Viso Del Marqués is defined by the Sierra Morena mountain range, Despeñaperros Natural Park, and diverse terrain with rolling trails. The network offers options primarily for experienced cyclists due to the difficult grading of routes. The routes in Viso Del Marqués are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 160 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Viso Del Marqués's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Viso Del Marqués?
A: Viso Del Marqués offers a selection of 12 touring cycling routes, primarily designed for experienced cyclists due to their challenging nature.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for touring cycling in Viso Del Marqués?
A: The region features diverse terrain, transitioning from the La Mancha plains to the foothills of the Sierra Morena mountain range. Cyclists can expect rolling trails, rugged mountains, and sun-soaked valleys, often with gravel sections, providing a challenging experience.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes in Viso Del Marqués?
A: Currently, all touring cycling routes listed for Viso Del Marqués are graded as difficult. The terrain, which includes rugged mountains and gravel sections, is more suitable for experienced cyclists. There are no easy or moderate routes specifically highlighted for families at this time.

Q: What kind of natural features or viewpoints can I expect to see on the routes?
A: The routes often traverse the beautiful Sierra Morena mountain range and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375490018/parque-natural-despenaperros-circular-de-gravel-desde-coto-sierra-del-viso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Despeñaperros Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;. You can find stunning natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Despeñaperros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of the landscape.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions accessible by bike?
A: Yes, some routes pass near or through areas with historical significance. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge in Despeñaperros – La Aliseda Recreation Area loop from Miranda del Rey&lt;/a&gt; includes the historic Roman Bridge. The region itself is home to the Palace of the Marquis of Santa Cruz and the Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción in Viso del Marqués, though direct cycling access to these specific sites may vary.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375490018/parque-natural-despenaperros-circular-de-gravel-desde-coto-sierra-del-viso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Despeñaperros — circular de gravel desde Coto Sierra del Viso&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge in Despeñaperros – La Aliseda Recreation Area loop from Miranda del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24523973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Aliseda Recreation Area – El Empedraillo loop from Santa Elena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Viso Del Marqués?
A: Given the region's sun-soaked valleys and location in southern Spain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling. Summers can be very hot, while winters might bring cooler, but often still rideable, conditions.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Viso Del Marqués?
A: While specific parking details for each route vary, many routes start from towns or recreational areas where parking facilities are available. For instance, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge in Despeñaperros – La Aliseda Recreation Area loop from Centro de Visitantes "Llanos de Las Américas"&lt;/a&gt; suggest starting points with potential parking options.

Q: Are there any waterfalls along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is home to beautiful waterfalls. You might encounter natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5289848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Negrillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Fraile Waterfall and Spring&lt;/a&gt;, which add scenic beauty to the landscape.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the touring cycling experience in Viso Del Marqués?
A: The touring cycling routes in Viso Del Marqués are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 5.0 stars from over 9 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging terrain and the stunning natural beauty of the Sierra Morena and Despeñaperros Natural Park.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the touring cycling routes?
A: While many outdoor areas in Spain are dog-friendly, specific regulations can vary, especially within protected areas like Despeñaperros Natural Park. It's advisable to check local park rules regarding dogs, particularly concerning leash requirements and access to certain trails, before heading out.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near small towns and recreational areas where you can find cafes, pubs, and accommodation. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2638820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Aliseda Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt; is a notable facility that could offer amenities. It's always a good idea to plan your stops in advance, especially on longer or more remote routes.

Q: What are some other notable landmarks near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore historical sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4072358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Empedraillo&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cimbarra Mill&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4215375" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Castillo de Castro Ferral&lt;/a&gt;. These offer glimpses into the region's rich history and culture.
tips:
To get to Puerto del Rey (1,132 m) we have to climb quite hard, also on a gravel road. This port was an important mountain pass during the Modern Age from the Plateau towards Andalusia.
It is located within the Despeñaperros natural park, a protected space, excavated by the Despeñaperros river. Resting after the good climb, you can enjoy the typical flora of the entire Sierra Morena, especially holm oaks, cork oaks and pines.

The El Empedraillo causeway is a historic path that dates back to Roman times. It is a stone path, carefully placed for the comfortable passage of horses. Today there is only a couple of hundred meters left, it is not signposted, so look carefully, so as not to miss this place with so much history under your feet.

These remains correspond to an old fortress perched on a promontory known as Castro Ferral.
This castle was demolished (it seems to be by the Castilian armies, during the battle of Las Navas de Tolosa that took place on July 16, 1212, to prevent it from falling into Muslim hands.
It has been like this for several centuries as a mute witness to the glorious historical past. Stepping on that terrain and touching those nearby rocks take us back to important moments in our history.
To climb it is necessary to make an effort, both on one side and on the other, by a steep firewall, although the reward for both the place and the views are well worth it.
